# Textwerk

> Textwerk is a friendly fork of Textual, the classic macOS IRC client, rebuilt to run cleanly on today's Mac (macOS 26 or later). It is free and open source.

Textwerk speaks modern IRCv3, connects over native IPv6, and supports SASL and client-certificate authentication. It works behind bouncers like ZNC and through SOCKS4/SOCKS5/HTTP proxies. The interface has native light and dark appearances, inline media previews, split-view and direct-message support, and built-in text-to-speech. It is scriptable with plain web standards plus Swift, Objective-C, Python, or AppleScript, and is fully keyboard-operable.

Textwerk is derived from Textual (BSD 3-Clause, © 2010-2020 Codeux Software, LLC) and LimeChat (BSD 2-Clause, © 2008-2010 Satoshi Nakagawa). It is not affiliated with or endorsed by Codeux Software.

Builds are unsigned: on first launch, users either right-click the app and choose Open, or clear the Gatekeeper quarantine flag with `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Textwerk.app`.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://textwerk.app/): product overview, feature list, screenshots, and install instructions
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/bashgeek/Textwerk): source code, issues, and discussions
- [Releases](https://github.com/bashgeek/Textwerk/releases): downloadable builds
- [Upstream project](https://github.com/Codeux-Software/Textual): the original Textual project this is forked from
